body_txt: Some people are amazing at stealth . These characters aren't . When a potentially deadly and effective strategy is ruined (or logically should be but comically isn't ) by the conspicuous way it is acted upon. Usually by a Battle Cry , an Attack Call , a Pre-Asskicking One-Liner , or something similar. Needless to say, characters who carry out this trope frequently cross into Too Dumb to Live territory, or at the very least aren't the sharpest tools in the shed. The calling card of Highly Visible Ninja . Compare So Much for Stealth when some small indication blows one's cover, Leeroy Jenkins when one idiot ruins the careful plans of the rest of the party, Starts Stealthily, Ends Loudly when some other factor means it's Time for Plan B , and Overt Operative who is terrible at espionage. Particularly spectacular versions of this trope may involve bursting through a nearby window or wall . Contrast with Noiseless Walker , who actually treads with catlike stealth. See also Subtlety Failure . In theatre, characters (especially in older works) may speak or even sing about how very silent and stealthy they're being. This can be Played for Laughs in some cases, but is more often simply treated as a dramatic convention, and the other characters can't hear them speak. Trope Namer is a song from The Pirates of Penzance , which is all about parodying this trope.
